name: Zanahary
description: Zanahary, also known as Andriamanitra, is the supreme creator deity in Malagasy mythology. He is considered the source of all existence, responsible for bringing the world and everything in it into being. Zanahary is seen as a distant and powerful figure, often associated with the sky and heavens, residing far above the earthly realm. While he is the ultimate creator, Zanahary is not typically directly involved in the daily lives of humans, instead delegating his responsibilities to lesser deities and spirits. He is the source of life, death, and fate, and ultimately controls the destiny of individuals and the world. Some myths describe him as having created humans from clay or earth, imbuing them with a soul or life force. Though not actively worshipped in the same way as ancestor spirits, respect for Zanahary is ingrained in Malagasy culture, acknowledging his role as the original creator and sustainer of the universe. Zanahary is often invoked in prayers for blessings and protection, particularly in times of great need or uncertainty. He represents the ultimate power and mystery of the cosmos, a force both benevolent and awe-inspiring. His name embodies the concepts of creation, power, and divinity, reflecting his central role in the Malagasy understanding of the universe.
subtitle: The Supreme Creator God of Madagascar

keyMyths.title: The Creation of the World
keyMyths.description: Zanahary created the world and everything in it from nothing. He shaped the land, the sea, and the sky, and filled them with plants, animals, and humans.

keyMyths.title: The Creation of Humans
keyMyths.description: Zanahary formed humans from clay or earth and breathed life into them. He gave them souls and the ability to reason, making them distinct from the animals.

keyMyths.title: Zanahary and the Ancestors
keyMyths.description: While Zanahary is the supreme creator, the ancestors play a more direct role in daily life. Zanahary is seen as the ultimate origin, while the ancestors mediate between him and the living.

sources.author: Maurice Bloch
sources.work: Placing the Dead: Tombs, Ancestral Villages, and Kinship Organisation in Madagascar
sources.description: Discusses the role of ancestors and the broader religious context in Madagascar.
sources.text: Discusses the role of ancestors and the broader religious context in Madagascar.

sources.author: Igor Kopytoff
sources.work: The African Frontier: The Reproduction of Traditional Societies
sources.description: Offers insight into Malagasy social structures and belief systems, which are relevant to understanding Zanahary's position.
sources.text: Offers insight into Malagasy social structures and belief systems, which are relevant to understanding Zanahary's position.

sources.author: Various
